source: Curated from Morgan Stanley, UBS, Citi HK graduate programme listings, HKIB ECF frameworks, Glassdoor HK, eFinancialCareers, CTgoodjobs, and talent.gov.hk (2025-2026)

roles.growthOutlook: Hong Kong remains Asia's premier IB hub. Highly competitive entry with ~80-100 hour weeks. Strong demand for Mandarin-speaking analysts handling China cross-border deals. Graduate programmes at Morgan Stanley, Goldman Sachs, Citi, UBS.

requiredSkills.description: Build 3-statement models, DCF, LBO, and merger models. The single most tested skill in IB interviews. Must be extremely proficient in Excel for model building.
Deep understanding of income statements, balance sheets, cash flow statements, and their interrelationships. Ability to analyze company performance from filings.
Proficiency in DCF, comparable company analysis, precedent transactions, and LBO valuation. Core technical competency tested at all levels.
Advanced Excel for financial modeling; PowerPoint for pitch books, marketing memoranda, and investment committee presentations. Both used daily.
Retrieve financial data, screen for comparable companies, and access research reports. Bloomberg certification valued.
Understanding of the M&A lifecycle: origination, due diligence, negotiation, closing, and integration.
Understanding of capital structures, cost of capital, WACC, dividend policy, and financing alternatives.
Increasingly valued for automating data retrieval and analysis tasks. Not required but differentiates candidates.
Articulate investment theses clearly in writing and verbally. Client-facing communication from day one.
Business fluent Mandarin is now essentially mandatory for HK investment banking roles due to the dominance of China-related deals.
Useful for HK local client relationships. Less critical at global banks than Mandarin.

roles.growthOutlook: Growing demand driven by SFC/HKMA regulatory requirements and increasing focus on operational resilience. Fintech and virtual banks creating new risk analytics roles. Steady career path with good work-life balance vs. IB.

requiredSkills.description: Build risk reports, dashboards, and perform quantitative analysis. Advanced functions and VBA for automation.
Understanding of credit risk, market risk, operational risk, and enterprise risk management (ERM) concepts. Knowledge of Basel requirements and ISO 31000.
Query risk databases and extract data for analysis and reporting. Increasingly required in risk analytics roles.
Automate risk reporting, perform statistical analysis, and build risk monitoring dashboards. pandas and matplotlib are key libraries.
Apply statistical methods to assess risk exposure, model loss distributions, and perform scenario analysis.
Understanding of Hong Kong regulatory framework including SFC rules, HKMA supervisory approach, and three lines of defence model.
Create risk dashboards and visual reports for senior management and regulators.
Understanding of internal control frameworks, control testing, and audit processes for risk management.
Essential for regulatory reporting, policy documentation, and communication with international stakeholders.
Important for communication with local business units and Hong Kong-based regulators.

roles.growthOutlook: HK is Asia's largest wealth management hub. Strong growth from Greater Bay Area wealth flows and family office expansion. Roles at private banks, retail banks, and independent wealth managers.

requiredSkills.description: Understanding of mutual funds, bonds, structured products, FX, insurance-linked products, and portfolio construction.
Build and maintain client trust, understand financial goals, and provide personalized advice. Strong interpersonal skills essential.
Create comprehensive financial plans covering investment, retirement, tax, and estate planning for HNW clients.
Prepare portfolio reviews, investment proposals, and client presentation materials.
SFC licensing requirements (Type 1, 4, 9). Must pass HKSI LE papers for regulated activities. Knowledge of suitability requirements and KYC/AML.
Access market data, research, and portfolio analytics tools for client servicing.
Ability to identify client needs, present solutions persuasively, and close business while maintaining ethical standards.
Essential for building rapport with local HK HNW clients. Most wealth management roles require fluent Cantonese.
Increasingly essential for serving mainland Chinese clients, the fastest-growing wealth segment in HK.
Required for documentation, product research, and communication with international product providers.

roles.growthOutlook: HK's 8 virtual banks and growing fintech ecosystem (WeLab, Airwallex, Futu) driving demand. HKMA fintech 2025 strategy creating regulatory sandbox opportunities. Blend of finance and tech skills highly valued.

requiredSkills.description: Build fintech prototypes, automate processes, and perform data analysis. Most common tech skill for fintech roles.
Query transaction data, build financial reports, and analyze customer behavior patterns.
Understanding of blockchain technology, smart contracts, and tokenomics. Relevant for crypto/digital asset fintech roles.
Integrate with payment gateways, banking APIs, and third-party financial services. Open API knowledge valued.
Understanding of payments, lending, wealth management, or insurance products. Domain knowledge specific to the fintech vertical.
Understanding of KYC/AML requirements, digital identity verification, and regulatory technology solutions.
Cloud-native fintech infrastructure. Understanding of cloud security and data privacy requirements for financial data.
Work in fast-paced agile fintech teams with rapid iteration cycles.
Essential for working in international fintech teams and understanding global fintech trends.
Valuable for fintech roles involving cross-border payments and Greater Bay Area expansion.

roles.growthOutlook: Regulatory compliance roles consistently growing in HK. SFC and HKMA continuing to strengthen oversight. Fintech, crypto regulation, and ESG compliance creating new specializations. Stable career with strong demand.

requiredSkills.description: Understanding of Securities and Futures Ordinance, HKMA supervisory policy, and SFC codes of conduct. Core compliance knowledge.
Anti-money laundering regulations, customer due diligence, suspicious transaction reporting, and sanctions screening.
Understanding of governance frameworks, board reporting, and three lines of defence model in financial institutions.
Prepare regulatory filings, respond to regulator inquiries, and maintain compliance records. Attention to detail essential.
Maintain compliance registers, prepare policy documents, and create monitoring reports.
Conduct compliance reviews, testing of controls, and gap analysis against regulatory requirements.
Understanding of HK Personal Data (Privacy) Ordinance and its implications for financial services.
Essential for drafting policies, communicating with international regulators, and understanding English-language regulations.
Required for reviewing Chinese-language documents, communicating with local regulators, and supporting Chinese-speaking clients and staff.
